findings.note: Section (b) drives the .js twin via the PAYLOAD env var rather than stdin, which is the path the hook command (node sdd-hook-guard.js --emit exit) uses under real Claude Code. The stdin-decode of the .js twin for Bash payloads is not exercised end-to-end; it relies on pre-existing, unchanged guard infrastructure (already used for file-tool payloads) plus the sh/python parity path that does read stdin. Non-blocking; AC-012/AC-013 remain satisfied by the combination of matcher-routing (section a) and guard-decision (sections b/c) evidence.
